expectations.expectation: Plan includes a header with Goal, Architecture, and Tech Stack sections
expectations.justification: The plan opens with three bolded header fields: '**Goal:** Add role-based access control (admin, editor, viewer) to the existing API so that endpoints enforce permission checks based on the authenticated user's role.', '**Architecture:** Extend the existing JWT auth middleware at src/middleware/auth.ts with a role-authorization layer. Each user row in PostgreSQL gains a role column (enum: admin, editor, viewer). A new authorize middleware factory checks the decoded JWT's role against the required permission for each route...', '**Tech Stack:** TypeScript, Express, PostgreSQL, JWT (jsonwebtoken), Jest, supertest'.

expectations.expectation: Tasks are bite-sized (2-5 minute implementation steps), not large multi-hour blocks
expectations.justification: The plan has 8 tasks, each focused on a single concern: Task 1 (migration, ~20 lines), Task 2 (role types and permission map, ~30 lines), Task 3 (extend JWT payload in auth.ts, ~30 lines), Task 4 (authorize middleware factory, ~15 lines), Task 5 (wire into routes with integration test), Task 6 (token generation, ~15 lines), Task 7 (admin endpoint, ~30 lines), Task 8 (full suite verification, no new code). Each creates or modifies 1-2 files with narrow scope.

expectations.expectation: Every task specifies exact file paths for files to Create, Modify, and Test
expectations.justification: Each task has a 'Files:' block with labeled actions. Task 1: 'Create: src/db/migrations/XXXXXX_add_role_to_users.ts, Test: tests/db/migrations/add_role_to_users.test.ts'. Task 3: 'Modify: src/middleware/auth.ts, Test: tests/middleware/auth.test.ts'. Task 4: 'Create: src/middleware/authorize.ts, Test: tests/middleware/authorize.test.ts'. Task 7: 'Create: src/routes/admin.ts, Test: tests/routes/admin.test.ts'. The summary table lists all 14 files with actions.

expectations.expectation: Tasks include complete runnable code — not placeholders like 'add validation logic'
expectations.justification: All tasks provide complete TypeScript implementations. Task 1: full migration with CREATE TYPE user_role AS ENUM and ALTER TABLE with up/down functions. Task 2: complete Role/Action types, ROLE_HIERARCHY, ROLE_PERMISSIONS, and canPerform function. Task 3: complete auth.ts with JwtPayload interface, Express Request augmentation, and authenticate function with role defaulting. Task 4: complete authorize factory. Task 6: complete generateToken. Task 7: complete adminRouter with validation and DB query. No placeholders found.

expectations.expectation: Tasks follow TDD structure: write test, verify fail, implement, verify pass
expectations.justification: Tasks 1-7 follow explicit TDD: 'Step 1: Write the failing test' (complete test code), 'Step 2: Run test to verify it fails' (e.g., 'FAIL with "Cannot find module"'), 'Step 3: Write minimal implementation' (complete source), 'Step 4: Run test to verify it passes' (e.g., 'Expected: PASS (10 tests)'). Task 5 honestly notes the integration test may pass immediately since it self-contains the middleware wiring. Task 8 is verification-only.

expectations.expectation: Exact test commands with expected output are included
expectations.justification: Every task includes specific jest commands with predictions. Task 1: 'Run: npx jest tests/db/migrations/add_role_to_users.test.ts --verbose / Expected: FAIL with "Cannot find module"' then 'Expected: PASS (2 tests)'. Task 2: 'Expected: PASS (10 tests)'. Task 4: 'Expected: PASS (13 tests)'. Task 5: 'Expected: PASS (12 tests)'. Task 8: 'Run: npx jest --verbose --coverage / Expected: All tests PASS' and 'Run: npx tsc --noEmit / Expected: No type errors'.

expectations.expectation: Includes an explicit verification or review step (quality gate, integration test suite, or manual review checklist)
expectations.justification: Task 5 includes a 12-test RBAC integration test suite using supertest that exercises GET/POST/PUT/DELETE for all three roles and unauthenticated requests. Task 8 is a dedicated verification step: 'Run all tests' with npx jest --verbose --coverage and 'Run TypeScript compiler check' with npx tsc --noEmit. Together these serve as an explicit quality gate.

expectations.expectation: Plan defines exactly three roles: admin, editor, viewer
expectations.justification: Task 2 defines: 'export type Role = "admin" | "editor" | "viewer"' as a TypeScript union type. ROLE_HIERARCHY: { admin: 3, editor: 2, viewer: 1 }. ROLE_PERMISSIONS maps exactly these three roles. Task 1 creates the PostgreSQL enum: 'CREATE TYPE user_role AS ENUM ("admin", "editor", "viewer")'. Task 7 validates: 'new Set(["admin", "editor", "viewer"])'. The Permission Matrix at the end lists exactly three roles.

expectations.expectation: Plan's permission model gives admin full access, editor create+edit but not delete, viewer read-only
expectations.justification: Task 2 ROLE_PERMISSIONS: 'admin: new Set(["create", "read", "update", "delete"]), editor: new Set(["create", "read", "update"]), viewer: new Set(["read"])'. Tests verify: admin can create/read/delete, 'denies editor delete' (canPerform("editor","delete") === false), editor can create/update, 'denies viewer create/update/delete'. Task 5 integration tests confirm HTTP 403 for editor DELETE and viewer POST/PUT/DELETE. The Permission Matrix table shows admin=Yes for all, editor=No for delete, viewer=Yes only for read.

expectations.expectation: Plan includes a database migration for roles (new table or column)
expectations.justification: Task 1 is entirely dedicated to the migration. It creates src/db/migrations/XXXXXX_add_role_to_users.ts with up() creating 'user_role' enum type and adding 'role user_role NOT NULL DEFAULT "viewer"' column, and down() dropping the column and type. Tests verify the column exists with the correct default and that rollback works cleanly.

expectations.expectation: Plan modifies or extends the existing auth middleware at src/middleware/auth.ts
expectations.justification: Task 3 is titled 'Extend the JWT Payload Type to Include role' with 'Modify: src/middleware/auth.ts' in its Files block. The implementation adds JwtPayload interface with role: Role, imports Role from '../auth/roles', augments Express Request with user?: JwtPayload, and modifies authenticate to extract role from JWT with fallback: 'role: decoded.role || "viewer"'. Tests verify req.user includes the role field and defaults to 'viewer' when absent.

eval_feedback: The with_skill plan achieves a perfect score, passing all 7 structural expectations and all 4 domain-specific expectations. It is the strongest result across all 6 eval conditions.

The plan includes 47 total tests across 7 test files, covering unit tests, middleware integration, route integration, and full suite verification with coverage. The TDD structure is consistently applied.

The Permission Matrix summary table at the end provides a clear, reviewable reference for the access control model. The plan also honestly notes when a TDD step may not produce a red-green cycle (Task 5).
